title: Nahar Singh Mahal
tagline: Nahar Singh Mahal — heritage city of Haryana

overview.short: Nahar Singh Mahal is located at Ballabhgarh in Faridabad district of Haryana.
overview.description: Nahar Singh Mahal is located at Ballabhgarh in Faridabad district of Haryana. This fort was built by the forefathers of Raja Nahar Singh around 1739 AD, and after whom Ballabgarh was named, the construction however continued in parts till about 1850. The fort is also known as Raja Nahar Singh Palace.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Raja Nahar Singh (Ballabhgarh) metro station
topPlaces.description: The Raja Nahar Singh metro station (formerly known as Ballabhgarh metro station) is terminating station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. Finance Minister of Haryana urged to name Ballabhgarh Metro station after martyr Raja Nahar Singh.

topPlaces.name: Ballabhgarh
topPlaces.description: Ballabgarh, officially Balramgarh, is a large town, nearby Faridabad city and a tehsil (subdistrict) in Faridabad district of Haryana, India, and is part of the Delhi National Capital Region or Delhi NCR. The town was founded by Raja Balram Singh, in 1739, who also built the Nahar Singh Mahal palace in the same year. Raja Nahar Singh (1823–1858) was the last king of the princely state.

topPlaces.name: Sant Surdas (Sihi) metro station
topPlaces.description: The Sant Surdas (Sihi) metro station (formerly known as NCB Colony (Good Year) metro station) is located on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro in Faridabad, Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Escorts Mujesar metro station
topPlaces.description: Escorts Mujesar is an elevated station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. It is located after Bata Chowk station located in Faridabad, as of 6 September 2015.

topPlaces.name: Faridabad Thermal Power Station
topPlaces.description: Faridabad Thermal Power Station is located in the New Industrial Township of the Indian city of Faridabad, 30 kilometres (19 mi) from the capital Delhi. The power plant is one of the coal-based power plants owned by Haryana Power Generation Corporation Limited.

topPlaces.name: Faridabad Combined Cycle Power Plant
topPlaces.description: Faridabad Combined Cycle Gas Power Plant or NTPC Faridabad is located at Faridabad, in Faridabad district in the Indian state of Haryana. The power plant is one of the gas-based power plants owned by NTPC. The gas for the power plant is sourced from GAIL HBJ Pipeline.

topPlaces.name: Bata Chowk metro station
topPlaces.description: Bata Chowk is an elevated station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. It is located between Neelam Chowk Ajronda and Escorts Mujesar station.

topPlaces.name: Nahar Singh Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Nahar Singh Stadium, previously known as Mayur Stadium, is a cricket stadium in the Indian city of Faridabad. The last official match played here was an ODI between India and England on 31 March 2006.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Raja Nahar Singh (Ballabhgarh) metro station & around

items.note: The Raja Nahar Singh metro station (formerly known as Ballabhgarh metro station) is terminating station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. Finance Minister of Haryana urged to name Ballabhgarh Metro station after martyr Raja Nahar Singh.

Ballabgarh, officially Balramgarh, is a large town, nearby Faridabad city and a tehsil (subdistrict) in Faridabad district of Haryana, India, and is part of the Delhi National Capital Region or Delhi NCR. The town was founded by Raja Balram Singh, in 1739, who also built the Nahar Singh Mahal palace in the same year. Raja Nahar Singh (1823–1858) was the last king of the princely state.
The Sant Surdas (Sihi) metro station (formerly known as NCB Colony (Good Year) metro station) is located on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro in Faridabad, Haryana.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Escorts Mujesar metro station & around

items.note: Escorts Mujesar is an elevated station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. It is located after Bata Chowk station located in Faridabad, as of 6 September 2015.

Faridabad Thermal Power Station is located in the New Industrial Township of the Indian city of Faridabad, 30 kilometres (19 mi) from the capital Delhi. The power plant is one of the coal-based power plants owned by Haryana Power Generation Corporation Limited.

Faridabad Combined Cycle Gas Power Plant or NTPC Faridabad is located at Faridabad, in Faridabad district in the Indian state of Haryana. The power plant is one of the gas-based power plants owned by NTPC. The gas for the power plant is sourced from GAIL HBJ Pipeline.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Bata Chowk metro station & around

items.note: Bata Chowk is an elevated station on the Violet Line of the Delhi Metro. It is located between Neelam Chowk Ajronda and Escorts Mujesar station.

The Nahar Singh Stadium, previously known as Mayur Stadium, is a cricket stadium in the Indian city of Faridabad. The last official match played here was an ODI between India and England on 31 March 2006.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nahar Singh Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nahar Singh Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nahar Singh Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (32 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nahar Singh Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nahar Singh Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nahar Singh Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Nahar Singh Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nahar Singh Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Raja Nahar Singh (Ballabhgarh) metro station, Ballabhgarh, Sant Surdas (Sihi) metro station, Escorts Mujesar metro station, Faridabad Thermal Power Station.
